title: PM Operating System Update
objective: Explain current product operating progress, top risks, and the next decisions needed using a modern concise deck.
decision_required: Approve the current presentation-system scope and publishing model.
delivery_context: Quarterly leadership review for ProductOS operating-system maturity.
narrative_goal: Lead with the decision, back it with operating evidence, and show the publish-safe path for the current presentation system.
success_outcome: Leadership should approve the current presentation-system investment and understand why this is a trust-and-execution upgrade rather than cosmetic work.
audience_constraints: Keep the deck self-explanatory without relying on a live presenter.
Make risks and implementation boundaries explicit.
required_objections: Why is stronger styling alone insufficient?
How do we keep better storytelling from overstating certainty?
non_negotiables: Do not hide risk or confidence limits.
Do not let rendered slides become the source of truth.

slide_outlines.title: PM Operating System Update
slide_outlines.audience_question: What decision should leadership make about the presentation capability now?
slide_outlines.claim: The presentation system should be funded as a narrative rendering system, not treated as a visual polish pass.
proof_requirements: Show why the current architecture, not just the current styling, is the real constraint
slide_outlines.core_message: The presentation system should behave like a narrative rendering system, not a prettier slide generator.
slide_outlines.speaker_notes: Open with the point of view: the current output is structurally thin, so better styling alone will not fix it.
slide_outlines.visual_direction: Hero statement with restrained supporting signals.

slide_outlines.title: Where We Are
slide_outlines.audience_question: Do we already have enough product context and source material to generate stronger decks?
slide_outlines.claim: ProductOS already has enough structured operating material; the quality loss happens in evidence packaging and story planning.
proof_requirements: Show that upstream source artifacts already exist
Show where the current presentation pipeline compresses or drops useful structure
slide_outlines.core_message: ProductOS already has the raw material for strong presentations, but the current pipeline compresses it too early.
key_points: Structured operating outputs are now implemented
Upstream discovery and framing layers are in place
slide_outlines.cta: Align on the architectural diagnosis before debating styling.
slide_outlines.speaker_notes: Show that the platform already produces enough structure; the missing step is narrative planning and richer composition.

slide_outlines.title: What Still Needs Work
slide_outlines.audience_question: What would make a stronger presentation engine trustworthy rather than dangerously persuasive?
slide_outlines.claim: Narrative upgrades are only acceptable if publish controls keep evidence, uncertainty, and export tradeoffs visible.
proof_requirements: Show where publish controls are still thin
Show why confidence and fidelity treatment must stay explicit
slide_outlines.core_message: The current presentation stack lacks narrative planning, evidence packaging, and publish controls.
slide_outlines.cta: Treat publish checks and fidelity controls as part of the current presentation-system scope.
slide_outlines.speaker_notes: Make the system risks explicit so the current investment reads as a quality and trust upgrade, not cosmetic work.
slide_outlines.visual_direction: Risk matrix with visible confidence cue.

facts.statement: Structured operating outputs are now implemented across leadership, communication, and delivery layers.
Leadership approval is needed to fund the presentation system as a narrative rendering capability.
Portfolio visualization and cockpit-mode coverage are still early and should not be overstated.
PPT export fidelity still depends on simpler composition mappings than HTML.
Better styling alone will not close the current gap because the story and evidence layers are still thin.
Narrative upgrades require publish controls so uncertainty and blockers remain visible in executive decks.
